items.title: Man City to appeal rule barring Guéhi from final
items.summary: Manchester City are set to ask the English Football League for special permission to play Marc Guéhi in the Carabao Cup final, according to Pep Guardiola.

items.title: City fly past Newcastle into cup final with Arsenal
items.summary: Manchester City struck three times in the first half as they eased into the Carabao Cup final by completing a comfortable 5-1 aggregate win over Newcastle United.

items.title: Sources: Unhappy Ronaldo to boycott 2nd game
items.summary: Al Nassr star Cristiano Ronaldo will maintain his stance and boycott a second-straight league game after not receiving a concrete guarantee that the Saudi Arabia PIF will make changes regarding the Saudi Pro League club's management, sources told ESPN.

items.title: More questions than answers as Newcastle yet to catch fire
items.summary: Holders Newcastle United were knocked out of the Carabao Cup by Manchester City on Wednesday night and a challenging season shows no sign of relenting.

items.title: Grab your popcorn -- it's Man City vs. Arsenal for the Carabao Cup, and maybe more
items.summary: Man City's win in the Carabao Cup semis sets the stage for a final vs. Arsenal, and a potentially thrilling end to the season.

items.title: What wobble? Arsenal break streak to reach first final in six years
items.summary: Arsenal will play in their first cup final in six years, aiming to put their recent lack of silverware behind them.

items.title: Is Spurs' Moore the answer to Rangers' open-play issues?
items.summary: Consistency is the next step for Rangers winger Mikey Moore after a standout performance against Kilmarnock, says head coach Danny Rohl.

items.title: 'Pure logic' Guehi should play in final - Guardiola seeks rule change
items.summary: Manchester City boss Pep Guardiola wants the English Football League to change their rules to allow Marc Guehi to play in the Carabao Cup final against Arsenal.

items.title: Hibernian 3-2 Dundee United
items.summary: Highlights from the Scottish Premiership match between Hibernian and Dundee United

items.title: Bosz genoot van PSV en zag 'een van de slechtste wedstrijden' van Veerman
items.summary: Peter Bosz genoot woensdagavond van zijn PSV, dat tegen SC Heerenveen de halve finale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker bereikte. De trainer zag een sterke wedstrijd van zijn ploeg, waarin Paul Wanner uitblonk en Joey Veerman juist niet op zijn best was, volgens Bosz.

items.title: PSV blijft in de race voor alle drie de prijzen
items.summary: Op woensdagavond stonden er opnieuw twee wedstrijden in de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker op het programma. PSV speelde in eigen huis tegen SC Heerenveen. NEC kreeg bezoek van FC Volendam.

items.title: Hierin ontwikkelde Veerman zich tot sterspeler van PSV
items.summary: Joey Veerman is bezig aan een indrukwekkend seizoen bij PSV. Na de zege tegen SC Heerenveen in de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker werd hem duidelijk gemaakt waarin hij de afgelopen jaren de meeste progressie heeft geboekt.

items.title: Scottish Premiership roundup: Rangers close gap to Hearts at top by thrashing Kilmarnock
items.summary: Hibs’ late show stuns Dundee United; Falkirk win again Aberdeen v Celtic and Dundee v Motherwell postponed Rangers closed the gap on the Scottish Premiership leaders Hearts to three points with a 5-1 win against Kilmarnock but it took time to subdue the 10-man visitors at Ibrox. Hearts lost 1-0 at St Mirren on Tuesday night to open the door, while Celtic’s game at Aberdeen on Wednesday night was postponed. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Op dit moment is het verschil tussen PSV en de rest van Nederland te groot'
items.summary: Mario Been en Arnold Bruggink hebben genoten van het optreden van PSV in de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker . De Eindhovenaren waren na de ruime zege op Feyenoord van het weekend ook een paar maatjes te groot voor SC Heerenveen.

items.title: Veldman vindt berusting in bekeruitschakeling: 'PSV is meerdere maten te groot'
items.summary: Robin Veldman kon woensdagavond nog aardig leven met de bekeruitschakeling van SC Heerenveen. PSV was domweg een maatje te groot, erkende de trainer van de Friezen. Van boosheid of teleurstelling was dan ook weinig sprake.

items.title: Manchester City set up Arsenal final after Marmoush double sinks Newcastle
items.summary: Strap yourself in for a Wembley showdown between Manchester City and Arsenal that should be as fascinating on the grass as the sideline where Pep Guardiola and Mikel Arteta bid to outsmart each other. City’s manager was the Arsenal No 1’s tutor from 2016 to 2019 when Arteta was his coach. This forged a friendship, but there may be fireworks between them after Guardiola was incensed by the Spaniard’s claim that he had “all the information” on City after they complained about Arsenal’s “dark arts” tactics in the 2–2 draw at the Etihad Stadium last season. Continue reading...

items.title: Bartley's Livingston side beaten by Falkirk on debut in dugout
items.summary: Marvin Bartley's debut in the Livingston dugout ended in a 2-1 defeat at home to Falkirk in a William Hill Premiership clash at the Home of the Set Fare Arena.

items.title: Five-star Rangers thump Kilmarnock to cut Hearts' lead to three points
items.summary: Rangers moved up to second in the Scottish Premiership and cut leaders Hearts' advantage to three points with a 5-1 win over Kilmarnock at Ibrox.

items.title: Wizards acquire Davis from Mavericks in eight-player trade
items.summary: The Washington Wizards are acquiring Anthony Davis from the Dallas Mavericks in an eight‑player trade that also sends multiple draft picks to Dallas, according to a person close to the deal.

items.title: O'Neill on nerves of Celtic return and 'unconvincing' recruitment - but positive window
items.summary: Returning Celtic manager Martin O'Neill was worried about being considered "too old" to lead the club before returning for a second and then third spell earlier this season, and suggested he was unconvinced about their recruitment under former transfer chief Paul Tisdale.
